GuiSubtitle: Windows XP graphical interface, unlimited jobs, CryptoSoft encryption and business software blocking
CliDuplicateIndexes: Duplicate job indexes are not allowed.
CliIndexOutOfRange: Job indexes must be between 1 and {max}.
CliJobDoesNotExist: One or more requested backup jobs do not exist.
TargetDirectoryCreationFailed: The target directory could not be created.
BackupJobIndexOutOfRange: Backup job index is out of range.
DashboardSubtitle: High-level view of jobs, current state, encryption settings and business software blocking rules.
BusinessSoftwareAlertActive: Backups will be blocked if one of the following processes is detected: {0}
BusinessSoftwareAlertInactive: No business process is configured. No preventive block will be applied.
NoPauseMessage: Pause, resume and manual stop are not available in EasySave 2.0. This interface stays within the exact deliverable 2 scope.
JobsPageSubtitle: Review saved jobs and start executions without changing the current business logic.
ModifyDeleteUnavailableMessage: Edit and delete actions are not connected to the current ViewModel. The buttons remain disabled to avoid inventing risky business logic.
CreatePageSubtitle: Provide a job name, a source folder, a target folder and the expected copy mode.
EditJobPageSubtitle: Create a new job or load an existing one from the list to edit it safely.
EditJobSourceHint: Choose the folder containing the files to back up.
FormGuidanceBody: Use a short name, select an exact source folder, then a reachable target folder. The browse buttons reuse the existing Avalonia folder picker.
CompleteDescription: Copies the full source tree to the destination.
DifferentialDescription: Copies only missing, changed or size-different files.
ExecutionPageSubtitle: Monitor progress for the selected job and launch a single or sequential execution.
ExecutionBusinessSoftwareDetected: Business software detected: {0}. Backup launch or continuation is blocked.
ExecutionBusinessSoftwareSafe: No blocking business software is detected.
ExecutionRunningNote: The selected job is currently running. Pause takes effect after the current file, while Stop interrupts the current task.
ExecutionPausedNote: The selected job is paused. Use Play to resume the transfer flow.
ExecutionStoppedNote: The selected job is stopped. Use Play to start it again from a new execution.
ExecutionControlReadyNote: The selected job is ready. Use Play to start, Pause after launch, or Stop to cancel a running execution.
LogsPageSubtitle: Preview existing JSON or XML files without changing EasyLog or the logging engine.
LogsPreviewEmpty: No log has been generated yet for the selected format.
LogsPreviewHint: Logs will appear here after a backup execution.
LogsPreviewError: Unable to read the log preview.
StatePageSubtitle: Direct read of state.json through the existing project logic.
SettingsPageSubtitle: Configure language, logs, CryptoSoft and the business software blocking rule.
SettingsLogHint: JSON is recommended for modern monitoring dashboards.
SettingsHelpBody: Project documentation covers CryptoSoft configuration, log formats and business software blocking behavior.
SettingsGuideSubtitle: This page explains every button, field and exact behavior available in the Settings screen.
SettingsGuideApplyBody: The Apply button immediately saves the values shown in the Settings screen. It updates the settings.json file used by the application, reloads the language if it changed, then reuses these values for the next backups.
SettingsGuideLanguageBody: The Language field changes the interface texts. It does not alter the backup data itself, but it reloads the GUI labels after saving.
SettingsGuideLogFormatBody: The Log format field chooses whether future logs are written as JSON or XML. This setting affects the next log files generated in the logs folder.
SettingsGuideStatePathBody: State file path: read-only field. It shows where EasySave writes state.json during a backup to track progression in real time.
SettingsGuideLogsPathBody: Logs directory: read-only field. It shows the folder where daily JSON or XML log files are generated after copies.
SettingsGuideSettingsPathBody: Settings file path: read-only field. It shows the exact location of settings.json, meaning the file that stores language, log format, CryptoSoft, the encryption key and encrypted extensions.
SettingsGuideCryptoPathBody: CryptoSoft path: points to the executable or folder used by CryptoSoft. EasySave uses it to encrypt copied files after backup.
SettingsGuideCryptoKeyBody: Encryption key: text sent to CryptoSoft to encrypt files. With the same key, the same algorithm can transform the file back.
SettingsGuideExtensionsBody: Encrypted extensions: list of extensions separated by ;. If you enter *, EasySave tries to encrypt every copied file. If you enter .txt;.jpg, only those file types are encrypted.
SettingsGuidePriorityExtensionsBody: Priority extensions: list of extensions separated by ;. If a priority extension is still waiting in any running job, EasySave does not start any non-priority file on any other job until all pending priority files are processed.
SettingsGuideLargeFileThresholdBody: Large file threshold (Ko): if a file is strictly larger than this value, EasySave allows only one such transfer at a time across all running jobs. Smaller files can still transfer in parallel if the priority rule allows it.
SettingsGuideBusinessBody: The business process field contains process names separated by ;, for example calc;notepad. If EasySave detects one of these processes, it blocks the backup sequence, writes a blocking event to the log and marks the state as Blocked.
SettingsGuideBehaviorBody: All fields on this page prepare the next execution. State and settings paths are informational, while language, log format, CryptoSoft, the key and encrypted extensions directly affect how the next backups run.
SettingsGuideReminderBody: Practical reminder: changing a value on the screen is not enough. You must click Apply to save the changes. Otherwise the previous values remain in use.
SettingsCryptoBody: Only configured extensions are sent to CryptoSoft. Priority extensions are processed before non-priority files across parallel jobs. Path and key stay stored in the existing settings file.
SettingsBusinessBody: If a configured process is detected, EasySave finishes the current file, writes the block to the log, then stops the sequence. The large file threshold limits simultaneous transfers for files bigger than the configured Ko value.
AboutPageSubtitle: Compact overview of the ProSoft professional backup product.
AboutPriceValue: EUR 200 excl. tax - annual maintenance 12% of purchase price

SelectJobHint: Select a job to display execution details.
